assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response mentions update frequency differences but doesn't explain SLA differences or minor vs patch version distinctions between channels

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states that 'The channel switch triggers a master upgrade' and mentions API server unavailability, contradicting this expectation

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: While response mentions checking if 1.32 is available in Stable, it doesn't warn about missing patches if the version isn't available yet

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Provides the correct gcloud container clusters update command with --release-channel=stable parameter

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends using gcloud container get-server-config to check available Stable versions before switching

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Explains that update frequency will change from every 2-4 weeks to every 2-3 months and mentions node pool upgrade timing

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Extended channel is not mentioned as an option for even slower update cadence

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends planning for a maintenance window and scheduling during low-traffic periods
